mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year walk-forward test, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate revenues and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or null predictive accuracy over rolling 5-year windows, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect supply chain normalization and capacity utilization that alleviate input cost pressures, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 235.0 signal entrenched inflation that forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in over 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the threshold effect shows no statistical edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment rates confirm broad-based labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes insignificant when adjusted for sector rotation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated input costs signals supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to maintain predictive accuracy across different inflation regimes.
